payload.title: Architect-builder ergonomics gaps observed during User Site capability work
payload.summary: During User Site operator-surface, Komorebi, and display-toggle work, several recurring ergonomics and coherence gaps appeared around builder handoff, review, task closure precision, current work visibility, and brittle upstream submission.
evidence: Builder completed and committed work but did not leave an explicit architect-facing review_request packet. Architect had to inspect commits, task files, logs, and repository state manually.

Task 024 was closed as preserving Komorebi grid slots across minimize/restore, but review found the implemented capability was manual save/restore plus fixture proof, not automatic preservation on the normal Alt-Tab restore path. A follow-up task 030 was needed.
The display button label 'Swap Displays' drifted from actual V1 semantics, which were dearbitrized to 'Toggle Primary Display'. UI labels need semantic tests and should not overpromise behavior.
Architect review created follow-up task 030, but that creates another lifecycle handoff question: review-generated tasks need a standard allocation/commit/handoff flow.
Narada proper submissions remain brittle without MCP. The current surface required WSL CLI, Node version discipline, and temporary scripts to avoid shell quoting failures.
The operator repeatedly had to ask where we were. Active chapters, pending reviews, builder-running tasks, architect-created follow-ups, and upstream submissions need a current workboard/read-model.
Architect and builder can work concurrently on adjacent task/chapter files without an explicit coordination protocol, creating risk of overlapping mutations or hidden responsibility transfer.
proposal: Add builder completion handoff/review_request requirements to role doctrine or task lifecycle templates: commits, changed files, verification artifacts, residuals, and requested review questions.
Improve task closure semantics so task status distinguishes proven manual helper, operator entrypoint, event-driven automation, and fully integrated capability.
Add semantic projection tests for operator UI labels/buttons, especially when UI labels name operations with potentially broader meaning than implementation.
Define a lifecycle path for architect-created follow-up tasks during review: allocation, task file creation, commit expectation, and routing to builder.
Provide a current workboard/read-model for active chapters, pending reviews, in-progress builder work, local follow-ups, and submitted upstream envelopes.
Add explicit architect-builder concurrency guidance for shared Site mutation: when simultaneous work is allowed, how files are partitioned, and how handoff/review crossings are recorded.
payload.recommendation: Treat as ergonomics/doctrine input adjacent to CAPA Operation, Intent Interpretation/Admission zones, canonical inbox scale-relative crossing metadata, and role-specific AI thread bootstrap.

promotion.note: recorded_pending_crossing: executable promotion for target kind 'decision' is not implemented yet.
